values.brief: The clipping mode of an actor.
values.name: CLIPPING_MODE

values.brief: The color of an actor.
values.name: COLOR

values.brief: The alpha component of an actor's color.
values.name: COLOR_ALPHA

values.brief: The blue component of an actor's color.
values.name: COLOR_BLUE

values.brief: The green component of an actor's color.
values.name: COLOR_GREEN

values.brief: The color mode of an actor.
values.name: COLOR_MODE

values.brief: The red component of an actor's color.
values.name: COLOR_RED

values.brief: The flag whether the actor is connected to the Scene. When an actor is connected, it will be directly or indirectly parented to the root Actor.
values.name: CONNECTED_TO_SCENE

values.brief: Returns whether the actor is culled or not.
values.name: CULLED

values.brief: The draw mode of an actor.
values.name: DRAW_MODE

values.brief: The flag to determine the height dependent on the width.
values.name: HEIGHT_FOR_WIDTH

values.brief: The resize policy for the height of an actor.
values.name: HEIGHT_RESIZE_POLICY

values.brief: The current depth in the hierarchy of the actor.
values.name: HIERARCHY_DEPTH

values.brief: The unique ID of the actor.
values.name: ID

values.brief: Determines whether child actors inherit the layout direction from a parent.
values.name: INHERIT_LAYOUT_DIRECTION

values.brief: The flag whether a child actor inherits it's parent's orientation.
values.name: INHERIT_ORIENTATION

values.brief: The flag whether a child actor inherits it's parent's position.
values.name: INHERIT_POSITION

values.brief: The flag whether a child actor inherits it's parent's scale.
values.name: INHERIT_SCALE

values.brief: The flag whether the actor is of class Dali::Layer.
values.name: IS_LAYER

values.brief: The flag whether an actor is the root actor, which is owned by the Scene.
values.name: IS_ROOT

values.brief: The flag whether the actor should be focusable by keyboard navigation.
values.name: KEYBOARD_FOCUSABLE

values.brief: The direction of the layout.
values.name: LAYOUT_DIRECTION

values.brief: The flag whether an actor should receive a notification when touch or hover motion events leave. In the case of the hover event, when the hover event enters the actor, it will receive started state.
values.name: LEAVE_REQUIRED

values.brief: The maximum size an actor can be assigned in size negotiation.
values.name: MAXIMUM_SIZE

values.brief: The minimum size an actor can be assigned in size negotiation.
values.name: MINIMUM_SIZE

values.brief: The name of an actor.
values.name: NAME

values.brief: The opacity of the actor.
values.name: OPACITY

values.brief: The orientation of an actor.
values.name: ORIENTATION

values.brief: The padding of an actor for use in layout.
values.name: PADDING

values.brief: The origin of an actor, within its parent's area.
values.name: PARENT_ORIGIN

values.brief: The x origin of an actor, within its parent's area.
values.name: PARENT_ORIGIN_X

values.brief: The y origin of an actor, within its parent's area.
values.name: PARENT_ORIGIN_Y

values.brief: The z origin of an actor, within its parent's area.
values.name: PARENT_ORIGIN_Z

values.brief: The anchor-point of an actor.
values.name: PIVOT

values.brief: The x anchor-point of an actor.
values.name: PIVOT_X

values.brief: The y anchor-point of an actor.
values.name: PIVOT_Y

values.brief: The z anchor-point of an actor.
values.name: PIVOT_Z

values.brief: The position of an actor.
values.name: POSITION

values.brief: Determines whether the pivot should be used to determine the position of the actor.
values.name: POSITION_USES_PIVOT

values.brief: The x position of an actor.
values.name: POSITION_X

values.brief: name "progress" type Array of float
values.name: PROGRESS

values.brief: name "value" type Array of float
values.name: VALUE

values.brief: name "controlPoints", type Vector3
values.name: CONTROL_POINTS

values.brief: name "points", type Vector3
values.name: POINTS

code_verification: receiver method exists on the receiver type
static methods are called statically and member methods are called on valid instances
assignment target is compatible with the method return type
argument count, argument type, and overload shape match the public API
enum owner and enum value both exist
property constants use the correct owner and value type
signal callback signatures match the public signal typedef
code blocks do not rely on inferred namespaces or overloads from naming alone
draft_requirements: Write enough practical detail for application developers to use the feature in a dali-ui app.
Ground natural-language behavior claims in the actual public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when the context pack is not enough.
Before writing examples, inspect the referenced public headers and relevant sample files when needed.
Prefer examples copied or adapted from existing samples when they match the feature; otherwise keep examples small and verify every API use.
After drafting, review the completed Markdown line by line against repository evidence and fix inaccurate explanations or code before returning.
repair_requirements: Repair only the problematic spans and the smallest surrounding context needed; do not rewrite the whole document.
For each listed issue, use the repository evidence and context pack to verify the corrected wording or code before returning.
If an issue reveals the same API misuse nearby, fix the nearby equivalent misuse too; do not make unrelated style rewrites.
After repair, re-check the edited lines against public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when needed.

rules: Use dali-ui symbols as the app-facing API surface.
Use dali-core and dali-adaptor symbols only as setup/background unless the usage pattern explicitly marks them preferred.
Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples.

usage_patterns.intent: Animate a View using the dali-ui app-facing animation API.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Use View as the object being animated. Inherited transform property enums are owned by Dali::Actor::Property.

rendered_prompt:
You are reviewing an existing DALi dali-ui application developer guide draft.
This is Phase 2.3.5: source-grounded prose review.

Primary goal:
- Improve natural-language accuracy and guide flow using repository evidence.
- Preserve the document's useful structure and examples whenever possible.
- Do not perform a full rewrite.
- Keep the revised user-facing guide in en only.
- Remove Korean, Japanese, Chinese, or other non-English prose from the revised guide.

Feature target:
- Feature: Animation (animation)
- Category: animation-motion
- Guide profile: dali-ui-app
- Audience: application developers using dali-ui public APIs

Review rules:
- Review every natural-language paragraph line by line against public headers, implementation files, samples, and UTCs when needed.
- Accuracy is the first priority. Flow is the second priority.
- Edit the smallest unit needed: word, sentence, paragraph, or section-local addition/removal.
- If a word-level change is enough, change only the word.
- If a sentence-level change is enough, change only the sentence.
- If an entire paragraph is based on a wrong premise, revise, add, or remove that paragraph.
- Preserve code blocks when possible.
- If prose can be corrected to match an already accurate code block, prefer changing prose.
- If the code block itself makes the prose inaccurate or breaks the guide flow, rewrite only the smallest necessary part of that code block and verify it against public headers/source.
- Do not invent APIs.
- Do not add generic filler just to make the document longer.
- Do not change section structure unless prose_review.allow_section_restructure is true or the current prose is clearly misleading.
- Prefer localized edits when prose_review.localized_edits_only is true.
- Preserve code blocks especially when prose_review.preserve_code_blocks is true.
- Prefer Dali::Ui::View, typed setters, fluent chaining, and view-tree idioms for app-facing guidance.
- Treat dali-core and dali-adaptor APIs as reference/context unless the feature explicitly requires them.

---
title: Animation
sidebar_label: Animation
category: animation-motion
---

# Animation

Animation in dali-ui provides a type-safe, fluent API for animating `View` properties. The animation system supports property transitions, key-frame animations, and reusable animation specifications.

## Table of Contents

- [Creating and Playing Animations](#creating-and-playing-animations)
- [View-First Animation with Bridge Pattern](#view-first-animation-with-bridge-pattern)
- [Reusable Animation Specifications](#reusable-animation-specifications)
- [Alpha Functions](#alpha-functions)
- [Key-Frame Animations](#key-frame-animations)
- [Animation Control](#animation-control)
- [Looping and Playback Range](#looping-and-playback-range)
- [Animation Signals](#animation-signals)

## Creating and Playing Animations

Create an `Animation` instance using `Animation::New()`, configure it, and call `Play()` to start the animation.

```cpp
// Create a 2-second animation
Animation animation = Animation::New(2.0f);

// Configure and play
animation.SetDefaultAlphaFunction(AlphaFunction::EASE_IN_OUT);
animation.Play();
```


## View-First Animation with Bridge Pattern

The preferred approach in dali-ui is to use `View::Animate()` which returns a `ViewAnimationBridge`. This provides a type-safe, fluent chaining API for animating View properties.

```cpp
// Create an animation and apply it to a View
Animation anim = Animation::New();
view.Animate(anim)
  .Opacity(0.0f, 500_ms, AlphaFunction::EASE_OUT)
  .PositionY(100.0f, 300_ms);

anim.Play();
```

The `ViewAnimationBridge` supports animating various View properties:

```cpp
view.Animate(anim)
  .BackgroundColor(UiColor::PRIMARY, 500_ms)
  .Opacity(0.5f, 300_ms, AlphaFunction::EASE_IN_OUT)
  .PositionX(50.0f, 200_ms)
  .PositionY(100.0f, 400_ms)
  .ScaleX(1.5f, 300_ms)
  .ScaleY(1.5f, 300_ms)
  .CornerRadius(Vector4(10.0f, 10.0f, 10.0f, 10.0f), 500_ms);
```

Use the `*By` variants to animate by relative amounts:

```cpp
view.Animate(anim)
  .OpacityBy(-0.3f, 300_ms)      // Decrease opacity by 0.3
  .PositionXBy(50.0f, 200_ms);    // Move right by 50 pixels
```

## Reusable Animation Specifications

For animations that need to be reused across multiple Views, create a `ViewAnimationSpec` using `View::NewAnimationSpec()`.

```cpp
// Create a reusable fade-in specification
ViewAnimationSpec fadeInSpec = View::NewAnimationSpec()
  .Opacity(1.0f, 500_ms, AlphaFunction::EASE_IN_OUT);

// Apply to any View with any Animation
Animation anim = Animation::New();
fadeInSpec.ApplyTo(anim, view);
anim.Play();
```

This pattern is useful for defining animation presets that can be applied consistently throughout your application.

## Alpha Functions

Alpha functions define the rate of change of an animation parameter over time. Use `AlphaFunction` to specify easing behavior.

### Built-in Alpha Functions

```cpp
AlphaFunction::LINEAR           // No transformation
AlphaFunction::EASE_IN          // Speeds up and stops suddenly
AlphaFunction::EASE_OUT         // Sudden start, slows to gradual stop
AlphaFunction::EASE_IN_OUT      // Speeds up and slows down
AlphaFunction::EASE_IN_SQUARE   // Square easing
AlphaFunction::EASE_OUT_SQUARE  // Square easing
AlphaFunction::EASE_IN_SINE     // Sinusoidal easing
AlphaFunction::EASE_OUT_SINE    // Sinusoidal easing
AlphaFunction::EASE_IN_OUT_SINE // Sinusoidal easing
AlphaFunction::BOUNCE           // Bouncing effect
AlphaFunction::EASE_OUT_BACK     // Exceeds end position then returns
```

### Setting the Default Alpha Function

Set a default alpha function for all animations in an `Animation` instance:

```cpp
Animation animation = Animation::New(1.0f);
animation.SetDefaultAlphaFunction(AlphaFunction::EASE_IN_OUT);
```

### Custom Bezier Curves

Create custom easing curves with bezier control points:

```cpp
// Control points for a custom curve
AlphaFunction customAlpha(Vector2(0.25f, 0.1f), Vector2(0.25f, 1.0f));
```

### Spring Animations

Use spring-based physics animations for natural motion:

```cpp
// Predefined spring types
AlphaFunction spring(AlphaFunction::GENTLE);  // Slow, smooth motion
AlphaFunction spring(AlphaFunction::QUICK);   // Fast settling
AlphaFunction spring(AlphaFunction::BOUNCY); // Highly elastic
AlphaFunction spring(AlphaFunction::SLOW);    // Relaxed motion
```

## Key-Frame Animations

Use `KeyFrames` to define complex animations with multiple values at specific progress points.

```cpp
KeyFrames keyFrames = KeyFrames::New();
keyFrames.Add(0.00f, Property::Value(Vector4(0.0f, 0.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f)));
keyFrames.Add(0.25f, Property::Value(Vector4(0.0f, 0.0f, 0.5f, 0.5f)));
keyFrames.Add(0.50f, Property::Value(Vector4(0.25f, 0.25f, 0.5f, 0.5f)));
keyFrames.Add(0.75f, Property::Value(Vector4(0.5f, 0.5f, 0.5f, 0.5f)));
keyFrames.Add(1.00f, Property::Value(Vector4(0.0f, 0.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f)));

Animation animation = Animation::New(3.0f);
animation.AnimateBetween(Property(view, View::Property::CORNER_RADIUS), keyFrames, AlphaFunction::EASE_IN_OUT);
animation.Play();
```

For animating properties inherited from `Actor`, use the `Actor::Property` indices:

```cpp
Animation animation = Animation::New(1.0f);
animation.AnimateTo(Property(view, Actor::Property::OPACITY), 0.0f, AlphaFunction::EASE_OUT);
animation.AnimateTo(Property(view, Actor::Property::SCALE), Vector3(1.5f, 1.5f, 1.0f));
animation.Play();
```

## Animation Control

### Play, Pause, and Stop

Control animation playback with `Play()`, `Pause()`, and `Stop()`:

```cpp
Animation animation = Animation::New(2.0f);

animation.Play();   // Start or resume the animation
animation.Pause();  // Pause the animation
animation.Stop();   // Stop and apply end action
```

### Play from Specific Progress

Start an animation from a specific progress point:

```cpp
animation.PlayFrom(0.5f);  // Start from 50% progress
```

### Play After Delay

Delay the start of an animation:

```cpp
animation.PlayAfter(0.5f);  // Start after 500ms delay
```

### Query Animation State

Check the current state of an animation:

```cpp
if(animation.GetState() == Animation::PLAYING)
{
  // Animation is currently playing
}
```

The `State` enum values are:
- `Animation::STOPPED`
- `Animation::PLAYING`
- `Animation::PAUSED`

### Current Progress

Get or set the current progress:

```cpp
float progress = animation.GetCurrentProgress();
animation.SetCurrentProgress(0.25f);
```

### Speed Factor

Adjust the playback speed:

```cpp
animation.SetSpeedFactor(2.0f);  // Double speed
animation.SetSpeedFactor(0.5f);  // Half speed
animation.SetSpeedFactor(-1.0f); // Play in reverse
```

## Looping and Playback Range

### Looping

Enable continuous looping:

```cpp
animation.SetLooping(true);
```

Or set a specific loop count:

```cpp
animation.SetLoopCount(3);  // Play 3 times
animation.SetLoopCount(0);  // Loop forever (same as SetLooping(true))
```

Query the current loop:

```cpp
int32_t currentLoop = animation.GetCurrentLoop();
```

### Looping Mode

Control how looping behaves:

```cpp
animation.SetLoopingMode(Animation::RESTART);      // Restart from beginning
animation.SetLoopingMode(Animation::AUTO_REVERSE);  // Reverse direction each loop
```

### Play Range

Limit animation to a specific progress range:

```cpp
animation.SetPlayRange(Vector2(0.2f, 0.8f));  // Play from 20% to 80%
```

## Animation Signals

Connect to the `FinishedSignal()` to be notified when an animation completes:

```cpp
animation.FinishedSignal().Connect(this, &MyClass::OnAnimationFinished);

void OnAnimationFinished(Animation& animation)
{
  // Animation has finished
}
```

## End Actions

Control what happens when an animation ends:

```cpp
animation.SetEndAction(Animation::BAKE);       // Save final property values
animation.SetEndAction(Animation::DISCARD);    // Discard property changes
animation.SetEndAction(Animation::BAKE_FINAL); // Save as if completed
```

Set the disconnect action for when animated objects are removed from the scene:

```cpp
animation.SetDisconnectAction(Animation::BAKE);
```

## Duration Literals

Use the user-defined literals for convenient duration specification:

```cpp
using namespace Dali::Ui;

Duration d1 = 300_ms;   // 300 milliseconds = 0.3 seconds
Duration d2 = 1.5_s;    // 1.5 seconds
Duration d3 = 2000_ms;  // 2000 milliseconds = 2 seconds
```

These literals can be used directly in animation methods:

```cpp
view.Animate(anim)
  .Opacity(0.0f, 300_ms)
  .PositionY(100.0f, 500_ms, AlphaFunction::EASE_OUT, 100_ms);  // With 100ms delay
